theme: designing casino floor routes that keep gamblers inside longest
title: Casino Floor Router: Surviving the Cashier
strategies: trivial: reproduce the checker's own reference construction -- mostly dump discretionary flow onto the tempting shortcut/return doors toward the high-cashier feature stations, only a small trickle keeps each loop going (scores ~0.1)

greedy: hold every cashier door at its mandatory floor (don't over-leak), then split the remaining discretionary flow EVENLY between the loop-continue door and the shortcut/return door at every pit/station -- a sensible per-row rule with no view of the whole cycle structure

strong: exploit that the Perron value is a cycle geometric mean, not a per-pit sum -- commit ALL discretionary flow to the low-cashier corridor loop (leaving every shortcut/return door at its bare floor), so the interior graph's dominant cycle never has to pass through a high-cashier feature station
invalid: emits a feature-station row with the return/ring/cashier doors below their mandatory floors (scores 0)
